dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the betrayal goes deeper than we thought. The traitor wasn't acting alone. There's a network of compromised officials.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} found correspondence showing that others have been making side deals with the {enemy_type_plural}. {quest_ally} says the whole thing around {quest_focus_the} was rotten from the start: people swearing oaths they never intended to keep. {quest_item} contains the full ledger.
dialogueChunks.outro: Get {quest_item} from {location_hint} before the conspirators destroy it. The betrayed need to see the full picture: not just one traitor, but a pattern.

dialogueChunks.intro: The traitor knows someone is investigating. They've restricted access and started destroying records.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} tried to access the evidence and was turned away. The traitor is burning documents: {quest_ally} saw smoke from the records room at midnight. If they destroy {quest_item}, the proof of {quest_threat_the} goes up in smoke.
dialogueChunks.outro: We have a narrow window to get {quest_item} out before the traitor finishes covering their tracks. There might be another way in {location_hint}.

dialogueChunks.intro: The person who committed the forgery has been identified. And they've fled to {location_hint}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} discovered that the forger has been altering documents for years. {quest_ally} found the original document hidden in a wall cavity, but the forger took {quest_item}: a seal that authenticates official records.
dialogueChunks.outro: Without {quest_item}, even the real document can be questioned. Track them down and recover the seal before {quest_threat_the} becomes permanent.
